title: TMT Discovery Route Recovery And Browse-Surface Performance
objective: Recover live TMT browse usability by eliminating the remaining timeout on `/api/v1/neta/tmt/facets` and `/api/v1/neta/tmt/frames`, using the already-landed read-only Supabase Data API browse helpers or smaller equivalent route-layer logic so TMT discovery becomes responsive enough to support downstream selector and browser parity work.
active_role: TCC Runtime TMT Browse Recovery Operator
dependency_note: Treat packets 010 and 011 as closed ETU-scope work. Packet 012 starts from the current local TMT/EMT REST browse checkpoint and must stay bounded to the TMT discovery timeout on facets and frames rather than reopening ETU control-path or delay-routing work.
required_inputs: Current local router checkpoint where TMT and EMT REST browse helpers are already landed in services/neta/router.py
Verified live probe split: `/api/v1/neta/tmt/context/1` and `/api/v1/neta/tmt/settings/1` return 200, while `/api/v1/neta/tmt/facets` and `/api/v1/neta/tmt/frames?limit=2` time out
The current demo-facing TMT route contract in demo/neta_tcc.html
constrained_outputs: Responsive `/api/v1/neta/tmt/facets` and `/api/v1/neta/tmt/frames` route behavior under the local live probe path
Focused route-side or helper-side changes only where they are required to remove the timeout
Focused validation proving TMT discovery is now usable for downstream selector work
A dated closure handoff under ops/agents/handoffs
No broad UI redesign and no unrelated ETU or EMT expansion
validation_steps: Start from the current local REST browse checkpoint rather than replacing it wholesale.
Make `/api/v1/neta/tmt/facets` and `/api/v1/neta/tmt/frames` return successfully under bounded live probes without widening into demo redesign.
Keep TMT context and settings behavior intact while repairing discovery performance.
Add or adjust focused validation for the repaired TMT discovery slice and record the exact probe or test evidence in the closure handoff.
Do not claim closure while the TMT discovery endpoints still hang or require broad manual workaround.
sql_permission_note: This packet authorizes read-side route, helper, demo-harness, and test changes only. It does not authorize database writes or schema migrations.
closure_note: Closure handoff recorded the bounded TMT browse recovery on 2026-04-25: `services/neta/router.py` now uses `_fetch_tmt_browse_rows_sql` to populate TMT browse rows through a single-CTE SQL path instead of the prior paginated REST cold-start; live probes show `/api/v1/neta/tmt/facets` and `/api/v1/neta/tmt/frames` healthy, with cold end-to-end 2.354 s and warm 214 ms, and the focused TMT route test slice passed.
